topic.what: Private blocklisting with approximate matching against proprietary blocklists while preserving privacy of both client inputs and server blocklist
topic.how: Three-party protocol (sender, receiver, server) with explicit check (expensive, evaluates against blocklist) and implicit check (fast, verifies against allowlist) using garbled circuits, OLE, and Fnpa
topic.why_matters: Prevents TOCTOU attacks in malware detection by separating expensive blocklist check from fast allowlist verification, enabling efficient email attachment screening without revealing content to third-party scanners

characteristics.what_it_measures: Latency for receiver to verify item was previously checked
characteristics.context: Single 100kB file with allowlist token verification

characteristics.what_it_measures: Client response time for embedding with reusable garbled circuits
Network traffic reduction from reusable garbled circuits
Explicit check response time by fuzzy hash type
characteristics.vs_baseline: TLSH outperforms ssdeep by ~3-5x and sdhash by ~10-45x
characteristics.context: TLSH chosen as optimal hash function

novelty: Formal Half-Moon Cookie definition with explicit+implicit check phases
Separation of embedding from blocklist check making performance sum not product
Reusable garbled circuit optimization reducing communication 1000x
Hiding-and-binding allowlist tokens enabling fast TOCTOU-resistant verification
Privacy preservation for both client input and server blocklist simultaneously
open_problems: Scaling to larger blocklists (10k+ entries) causes saturation at ~20 concurrent clients
Edit-distance metric compatibility requires additional embedding step
Storage growth linear with number of explicit checks
Server metadata leakage (Csnd-Crcv correspondence) inherent to architecture
Malicious server extension requires additional modifications
